message:
fix(indexer): clamp begin-concurrent env-var knobs to safe upper bounds

Three environment-variable tuning knobs on the begin-concurrent persist
path parsed user input, filtered `> 0`, and otherwise honored it:

  - CASS_INDEXER_BEGIN_CONCURRENT_RETRIES (default 6)
  - CASS_INDEXER_BEGIN_CONCURRENT_CHUNK_SIZE (default 32)
  - CASS_INDEXER_BEGIN_CONCURRENT_WRITER_CACHE_KIB (default 4096)

A typo like `=1000000` would then drive:
  - ~107 hours of retry wall time (retries × capped exponential backoff);
  - 1 000 000-row parallel chunks that thrash rayon;
  - ~30 GiB of per-writer SQLite cache across the parallel chunk pool.

None of these are defensive against a dev or operator mis-setting the
value — and the indexer silently burns CPU / memory with no feedback.

Extract a small `env_usize_bounded` / `env_i64_bounded` helper (kept in
the same `persist` module) that parses, filters, clamps to a constant
cap, and emits a structured `tracing::warn!` on clamp so an operator
who sets a pathological value can see it in the logs rather than
wondering where the wall time went.

Caps (chosen to cover every realistic workload):
  - retries: 32 (≈ 16 s worst-case inner retry wall time at 256 ms backoff)
  - chunk size: 512 (already in rayon-oversubscription territory on 32c)
  - writer cache KiB: 65 536 (≈ 2 GiB peak across the default 32 chunks)

Added `begin_concurrent_knobs_are_clamped_to_safe_caps` unit test to
lock in the contract. The 12 existing env-var parsing tests continue
to pass (they exercise values well inside the new caps).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
